subject: Chemistry
title: Aqua Regia: The Royal Water
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Aqua Regia: The Royal Water.

hook.text: Gold resists almost every acid. But mix hydrochloric and nitric acid together and gold dissolves like sugar in water.

buildup.text: Aqua regia means 'royal water' in Latin—named because it dissolves the 'king of metals.'

discovery.text: Nitric acid oxidizes gold atoms. Chloride ions from HCl stabilize them in solution, preventing the reverse reaction.

twist.text: In 1940, George de Hevesy dissolved two Nobel Prize medals in aqua regia to hide them from the Nazis.

climax.text: After the war, he precipitated the gold back out of solution. The Nobel committee recast the medals from the same gold.

punchline.text: Two acids that dissolve gold. Nobel medals hidden in a jar of liquid.

quiz.question: Why did George de Hevesy dissolve Nobel medals in aqua regia?
options: To hide them from the Nazis by turning solid gold into a liquid solution
To prove that aqua regia could dissolve any metal
To create a new gold alloy for scientific use
